title: 10 best herbal teas for fever

remedy_properties: Antipyretic compounds: Kansas coneflower tea contains antipyretic compounds, such as phenolic acids and flavonoids, which help to reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and reducing prostaglandin synthesis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to its rich content of polyphenols and terpenes, help to alleviate symptoms associated with fever, such as pain, swelling, and redness.

Immunomodulatory effects: Kansas coneflower tea has immunomodulatory effects, which means it can help regulate the immune response and reduce the severity of febrile illnesses by modulating the activity of cytokines and other immune cells.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as Kansas coneflower, teas helps with fever because it contains compounds that boost the immune system and reduce inflammation. The plant's roots and flowers have been traditionally used to combat infections, colds, and flu. The active ingredients, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, have been shown to stimulate the production of white blood cells, which helps to fight off invading pathogens and alleviate fever-related symptoms, promoting a faster recovery and overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These compounds help reduce fever by inducing fever-reducing cytokines and modulating the body's immune response.

Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help alleviate fever by exerting anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating effects, which aid in reducing the body's temperature and fighting off underlying infections.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): These polyphenolic compounds help decrease fever by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce the body's inflammatory response and alleviate fever.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia contain compounds that help reduce fever by stimulating the immune system and acting as an anti-inflammatory agent.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea angustifolia are used for their antiviral properties, which help alleviate fever by targeting the underlying cause of the illness.
Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are also used for their antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and fever by neutralizing free radicals in the body.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried echinacea angustifolia roots and flowers,.
Steep 2 tablespoons of the dried echinacea mixture in 1 cup of boiling water.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve to remove the solids.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey to the tea for sweetness,.
Serve the herbal echinacea tea hot or chilled,.

remedy_properties: Antipyretic: The flavonoids present in Maidenhair Tree tea have been shown to exhibit antipyretic properties, which means they can help reduce fever by blocking the production of prostaglandins, a key molecule responsible for triggering the body's thermoregulatory response.

Anti-inflammatory: The terpenes and volatile oils found in Maidenhair Tree tea possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with fever such as headaches, muscle pains, and joint stiffness by reducing inflammation in the body.

Immune-stimulating: The saponins present in Maidenhair Tree tea have been shown to stimulate the immune system, increasing the production of white blood cells that help fight off infections, which can lead to fever.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, teas helps with fever because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba have been shown to have a positive effect on the immune system, helping to reduce the severity of fever. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's ability to improve blood circulation and oxygenation can aid in the body's natural process of fighting off infections, making it a popular natural remedy for relieving fever and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds may help alleviate fever by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, thus contributing to a decrease in body temperature.
Terpenoids (specifically bilobalide): Bilobalide has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which could help mitigate fever by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
Ginkgolides: Ginkgolides have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate fever by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used to make teas for fever due to their medicinal properties and high flavonoid content.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make teas for fever due to their ability to help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with it.
Buds: The buds are sometimes used to make teas for fever as they contain bioactive compounds that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate fever.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and 500ml of boiling water for the tea base.
Measure 1 teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves per 8 ounces of water for the herbal infusion.
Steep the ginkgo biloba leaves in boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes to release the herbal properties.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the leaves.
Serve the herbal ginkgo biloba tea hot or iced within 30 minutes of preparation.

remedy_properties: Menthol content: Menthol, a key component of peppermint oil, has natural analgesic and antipyretic properties that help reduce fever by dilating blood vessels and improving circulation, allowing the body to cool itself more efficiently.

Antioxidant compounds: Peppermint tea contains a range of antioxidant compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help combat oxidative stress and inflammation caused by fever, reducing its severity and promoting recovery.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, can help reduce the body's natural response to infection, leading to a decrease in swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with fever.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, teas helps with fever because of its natural antipyretic and decongestant properties. The menthol content in peppermint tea helps to relax the muscles and ease the tension in the body, while its antiviral and antibacterial properties work to reduce the severity of the fever. Additionally, peppermint's expectorant properties help to clear the airways, making it easier for the body to breathe and recover from the illness. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from fever and cold symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene helps reduce fever by acting as a natural antipyretic, which means it lowers the body's temperature in response to infection or inflammation.
Menthol: As a key component of mentha x piperita, menthol has a cooling effect on the body, which helps to reduce fever and provide relief from discomfort associated with it.
Caffeic Acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate the symptoms of fever by reducing inflammation in the body, thus making it easier to recover.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Mentha x piperita for teas due to their high concentration of menthol, which helps to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with it.
Stems: Stems are also used for making teas as they contain menthol, which is released when the stems are crushed or bruised, providing relief from fever and other related conditions.
Flowers: Flowers are used to make teas for fever reduction as they contain menthol and other compounds that help to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms associated with fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves in the morning when the dew is still present on the plant.
Rinse the harvested leaves with cold water to remove any dirt or debris from the plant.
Chop the rinsed leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife to release their oils and flavor.
Steep one teaspoon of chopped mentha x piperita leaves in one cup of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the solids to enjoy the refreshing and invigorating flavor of mentha x piperita tea.

remedy_properties: Antiviral compounds: Elder tea contains antiviral compounds like flavonoids and iridoid glycosides, which help to combat viral infections that cause fever by inhibiting the replication of viruses.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder tea has anti-inflammatory compounds like polyphenols and saponins, which reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fever such as headache, muscle aches, and joint pain.

Mucolytic activity: Elder tea contains mucolytic agents that help to break down mucus and reduce congestion, making it easier to breathe and relieving symptoms of respiratory infections like the common cold and flu.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra, also known as elder, teas helps with fever because of its high concentration of flavonoids and organic acids, particularly sambunigrin and isorhapontigenin. These compounds have been found to have potent antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity and duration of a fever. The antiviral properties of Sambucus nigra tea can also help to combat the underlying viral infection causing the fever, while its anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe and calm the body, promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce the severity of fever and alleviate symptoms associated with viral infections.
Phenylethylamine (PEA): PEA is a natural decongestant and antipyretic that helps to reduce fever and relieve nasal congestion, making it easier to breathe and recover from illness.

Vacciniol glycosides (e.g., sambunigrin): These glycosides have been shown to have antiviral and immunomodulatory effects, which help to boost the immune system and reduce the duration and severity of fever.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are high in antiviral and antimicrobial compounds, making them effective against fever.
Leaves: The leaves contain bioactive compounds that help reduce fever and alleviate symptoms.
Buds: The buds are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and calm feverish conditions.
remedy_recipe: Gather 25g of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in a clean paper bag.
Boil 250ml of water in a saucepan and bring to a rolling boil.
Steep the sambucus nigra mixture in the boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Allow the tea to cool to a comfortable drinking temperature for 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Lavender's antiviral compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to reduce the severity and duration of viral infections, including fevers.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with fever, making it a natural remedy for alleviating symptoms like headaches and body aches.

Sedative properties: Lavender's calming effects, thanks to its sesquiterpenes and linalool content, can help to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety and stress, and improve sleep quality, which is often disrupted by fever.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, teas helps with fever because of its antipyretic and soothing properties. The tea's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation, which can help to lower body temperature. Additionally, the tea's calming effects can help to alleviate headaches and body aches associated with fever, promoting a restful night's sleep and a faster recovery. This makes English lavender tea a popular natural remedy for soothing fever and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, helping to reduce fever and alleviate discomfort.
Linalyl acetate: As a terpene, linalyl acetate has been shown to exhibit antipyretic and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate fever and reduce pain.
Lavandulol: This terpene has been found to possess antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce fever and alleviate discomfort caused by inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make teas for fever due to their antiseptic and antiviral properties.
Flowers: They are used to make teas for fever due to their calming and soothing effects, which can help reduce fever and promote relaxation.
Stems: They are used to make teas for fever due to their antiseptic and antiviral properties, similar to the leaves.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 dried lavandula angustifolia flowers or 2 tablespoons of fresh flowers for tea.
Steep the dried flowers in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes or 3-5 minutes for fresh flowers.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove loose flowers.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey or sugar to taste and stir the tea well.
Serve the tea hot or chilled immediately to enjoy the herbal benefits.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms like headaches, sore throats, and body aches associated with fever.
Antiviral properties: The antiviral properties of ginger tea may help combat the underlying viral infection causing the fever, reducing its severity and duration.
Expectorant properties: Ginger's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, relieving congestion and coughing associated with fever.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, teas helps with fever because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe the digestive system. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to have antipyretic effects, helping to reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms associated with fever. Curcuminoids: Although curcuminoids are more commonly associated with turmeric, some Zingiber officinale teas may contain trace amounts. Curcuminoids help reduce fever by exhibiting potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, thereby alleviating symptoms associated with fever.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Zingiber officinale are commonly used to make teas for fever due to their high concentration of active compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are used to make teas for fever as they contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fever.

remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh or dried zingiber officinale roots in the morning when the dew is still present.
Grind 1-2 teaspoons of the dried root or 3-4 inches of the fresh root into a fine powder.
Boil 1 cup of water in a kettle or on the stovetop for 5-7 minutes.
Steep the powder in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Strain the mixture and serve immediately to enjoy the herbal tea's unique flavor and aroma.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Slippery elm's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms such as headache, fatigue, and joint pain associated with fever.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make teas for fever because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce body temperature.
Barks: They are used to make teas for fever because they contain a compound called salicin, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: They are used to make teas for fever because they contain a compound called salicin, which helps reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 branches of fresh or 1-2 teaspoons of dried ulmus rubra leaves for 1 cup of tea.
Dry the harvested branches in a warm place for 2-3 days to remove excess moisture.
Grind 1 teaspoon of dried ulmus rubra leaves or 2-3 branches of fresh leaves into a fine powder.
Steep 1 teaspoon of ground ulmus rubra leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain and discard the solids to serve the herbal tea immediately.

remedy_properties: Antipyretic properties: Yarrow tea has been shown to have antipyretic effects, which means it can help reduce body temperature and alleviate symptoms associated with fever, such as headache and fatigue.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The active compounds in yarrow tea, such as sesquiterpene lactones, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain caused by infection or inflammation, making it an effective remedy for fever-related conditions like sinusitis and bronchitis.

Antioxidant properties: Yarrow tea is rich in antioxidants, which help neutralize free radicals and protect the body from oxidative stress caused by fever. This can also help mitigate potential damage to tissues and organs.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium, also known as yarrow, teas helps with fever because of its natural antipyretic properties. The herb contains flavonoids and terpenoids that have been shown to reduce inflammation and lower body temperature. The tea's antimicrobial properties also help to combat underlying infections that may be causing the fever. Additionally, yarrow tea has been traditionally used to promote sweating, which can help to cool the body and alleviate symptoms associated with fever. This makes Achillea millefolium a popular natural remedy for fever relief.
remedy_constituents: Caryophyllene oxide: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, helping to reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
Apigenin: As a flavonoid, apigenin has antipyretic and antispasmodic effects, which help to reduce fever and alleviate associated discomfort.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Achillea millefolium are used to make teas for fever due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves from a sunny spot with good air circulation.
Steep 1 teaspoon of the dried herb in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5 to 7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids.
Add honey or other natural sweeteners to taste if desired but not necessary for herbal tea.
Drink the tea immediately while still warm or let it cool to room temperature for later consumption.

remedy_properties: Decongestant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Eucalyptus oil has natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory compounds like eucalyptol, which can help relieve respiratory congestion and reduce inflammation in the body.

Antimicrobial Activity: The essential oils in Eucalyptus tea have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which may help combat infections that contribute to fever development and alleviate symptoms such as coughing and sneezing.

Expectorant Properties: The mucolytic properties of Eucalyptus oil can help break down mucus and promote its expulsion from the lungs, making it easier for the body to clear out excess phlegm and reduce congestion.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, teas helps with fever because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea also possesses expectorant properties, which facilitate the removal of mucus from the respiratory tract, thereby providing relief from cough and congestion associated with fever. remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol (1,8-Cineole): This terpene acts as a decongestant, reducing nasal congestion and making it easier to breathe, thus helping to alleviate the symptoms associated with fever.

Naringenin: This flavanone has antipyretic properties, which can help to directly reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and inducing the production of anti-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus leaves are commonly used to make teas due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a compound with decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: Eucalyptus bark is used to make teas for its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce fever and relieve respiratory issues.
Buds: Eucalyptus buds are used in teas for their high eucalyptol content, which can help alleviate congestion and reduce fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in early morning to capture optimal oil content and flavor intensity.
Dry the harvested eucalyptus globulus leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours to preserve potency.
Measure out 1 teaspoon of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves for every 8 ounces of boiling water.
Steep the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to release their essential oils.
Strain the tea and serve immediately to enjoy the refreshing and invigorating flavor of eucalyptus globulus tea.

remedy_properties: Citral: Citral, a compound found in lemongrass, has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fever.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in lemonggrass tea, such as citronellal and geraniol, have a cooling effect that can help bring down body temperature and provide relief from fever-related discomforts.
Antioxidants: Lemongrass tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may exacerbate symptoms of fever.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus, also known as lemongrass, teas helps with fever because of its natural antipyretic properties. remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Cymbopogon citratus to make teas for fever, as they contain high concentrations of essential oils with antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1/4 cup of cymbopogon citratus leaves and flowers and clean them thoroughly to remove dirt.
Boil 8 cups of water in a large pot for 10 minutes to create a hot water base.
Add the cleaned cymbopogon citratus leaves and flowers to the boiling water and let steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a large cup or teapot to remove solids.
Let the tea cool for 10 minutes before serving and enjoy the refreshing citrus flavor.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goat's rue teas helps with fever because of its natural antipyretic properties, which help to reduce body temperature. The tea contains compounds that inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are responsible for causing inflammation and increasing body temperature. Additionally, goat's rue has been traditionally used to treat respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, which often accompany fever. remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried galega officinalis leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow for optimal extraction of medicinal compounds.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids.
Add honey to taste and stir well before serving to enhance flavor and soothe the throat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme teas helps with fever because of its natural antiviral and antibacterial properties. The herb's essential oils, such as thymol and borneol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and ease congestion, making it an effective remedy for relieving cold and flu symptoms. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial agents help combat the underlying infection, promoting a speedy recovery and reducing fever duration. By sipping on a soothing cup of herbal thyme tea, individuals can find natural relief from the discomfort and misery associated with fever.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thymol, reduce inflammation in the body, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, including headache, sore throat, and fatigue.
Expectorant properties: Thyme's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion associated with a fever.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried thymus vulgaris leaves from a trusted source.
Combine one teaspoon of dried thymus vulgaris leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves.
Steep the herb mixture in 8 ounces of boiling water for five to seven minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh strainer and discard solids.
Serve warm tea immediately, or store cooled tea in refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with fever because of its natural antipyretic properties. Licoricidin: Possesses antipyretic properties, which can help lower body temperature by blocking the synthesis of prostaglandins, a type of hormone that regulates inflammation and fever response.
Flavonoids: Have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with fever, promoting recovery from illness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of licorice are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fever.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of licorice contain flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for soothing a fever.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in amounts of 2-3 teaspoons per serving.
Heat filtered water to a temperature of 212°f and let it cool down for 10 minutes.
Steep the dried roots in the cooled-down water for 5-7 minutes to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove any solids or sediment.
Allow the tea to steep further for an additional 5-10 minutes before serving hot.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold teas helps with fever because of its natural antipyretic properties, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve heat in the body. Anti-inflammatory properties: The tea's flavonoids and terpenes have anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation caused by fever and alleviate associated symptoms such as headaches and body aches.
Antimicrobial properties: Pot marigold contains antibacterial and antiviral compounds that can help combat infections responsible for fever, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties, making them effective in reducing fever.
Leaves: Pot marigold leaves are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which have natural cooling and calming effects on the body, helping to alleviate symptoms associated with fever.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried calendula flowers one tablespoon per cup of boiling water steep for three to five minutes.
Measure two teaspoons of loose-leaf calendula flowers into a heat-resistant teapot.
Boil fresh water and pour it over the calendula flowers allowing it to steep for ten to fifteen minutes.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup containing honey if desired.
Drink the warm calendula tea three to four times daily as needed for fever relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage teas helps with fever because of its natural antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as thujone and rosmarinic acid, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fever, including headaches and body aches. Additionally, sage's antimicrobial properties help combat the underlying infection that is causing the fever, promoting faster recovery and reducing the risk of complications. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage contains anti-inflammatory compounds like rosmarinic acid, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with fever such as headache, body aches, and joint pain.

remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried salvia officinalis leaves and steep them.
Boil one cup of water in a teapot for five minutes and then remove from heat.

Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to discard solids.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to fever that are treated with herbal teas are headaches, migraines, and body aches. Teas made from herbs such as willow bark, ginger, and peppermint have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and relieve discomfort. Other herbs like Echinacea and Goldenseal have immune-boosting properties that can aid in the recovery process. These teas can provide natural relief without the harsh side effects of over-the-counter medications.
intro_desc: Herbal teas for fever are a natural remedy that helps alleviate symptoms associated with fever, such as discomfort, fatigue, and congestion. They work by promoting sweating, reducing inflammation, and boosting the immune system. The best herbal teas for fever include Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Ginkgo biloba, Sambucus nigra, Eucalyptus globulus, Cinchona officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, Melissa officinalis, Achillea millefolium, and Glycyrrhiza glabra. If left untreated, fever can lead to dehydration, respiratory complications, and even organ damage, severely impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal teas that help with fever is a blend of Peppermint, Ginger, and Echinacea. Peppermint helps to relax and soothe the body, while Ginger's anti-inflammatory properties reduce fever and alleviate pain. Echinacea, on the other hand, is known for its immune-boosting properties that help to fight off underlying infections. Drinking a warm infusion of this combination can help to alleviate symptoms and support the body in its recovery process, promoting a speedy return to good health. Regular consumption may also prevent recurring fever.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.), a compound herbal tea preparation called Echinacea Plus was found to be effective in relieving fever and other symptoms of cold or flu in a study involving 95 participants. The study, which was conducted over a period of 90 days from January 1, 1999, to March 30, 1999, used a double-blind placebo-controlled design, where participants were randomly assigned to receive either Echinacea Plus tea or a placebo, and found a significant difference between the two groups, with a p-value of < 0.001. The results of this study suggest that herbal teas, such as Echinacea Plus, can be a useful natural remedy for fever and other symptoms of cold or flu, as they were able to relieve symptoms in a shorter period of time than a placebo.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas helps with fever because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. This enables the body to fight off infections and reduce the severity of fever. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe the body.
